name: [BUG-ANA-002] TC-ANA-002 — Analytics 405 body missing "message" field (inconsistent with all other APIs)
description:
**[BUG-ANA-002]**

The analytics API 405 body is:
```json
{"timestamp":"...","status":405,"error":"Method Not Allowed","path":"..."}
```

The dashboard and transaction APIs use:
```json
{"status":401,"error":"Unauthorized","message":"Missing Authorization token"}
```

The analytics 405 has `timestamp` and `path` but **no `message`** field.
The dashboard 401 has `message` but **no `timestamp` or `path`**.

**Expected:** All services must return `{status, error, message, timestamp, path}` for all errors.

**Impact:** Clients cannot reliably read `res.body.message` for analytics errors — they would get `undefined`.
descriptionHtml:
[BUG-ANA-002]

The analytics API 405 body is:

{"timestamp":"...","status":405,"error":"Method Not Allowed","path":"..."}

The dashboard and transaction APIs use:

{"status":401,"error":"Unauthorized","message":"Missing Authorization token"}

The analytics 405 has timestamp and path but no message field.
The dashboard 401 has message but no timestamp or path.

Expected: All services must return {status, error, message, timestamp, path} for all errors.

Impact: Clients cannot reliably read res.body.message for analytics errors — they would get undefined.
